answer:
**Manuṣmṛti (Book 1) does not depict cosmogenesis as "Brahma's exhalation"**—no such phrasing or motif appears in the text.[^1] Its central cosmogenic claim is instead a classic cosmic egg (hiraṇyagarbha) narrative: the universe arises from an initial state of unmanifest darkness, wherein the divine Self-Existent One (Svayambhū) manifests with creative power, produces waters as his first creation, places his seed in them to form a golden egg, is reborn within it as Brahmā (the grandfather of worlds), and from the split egg and his own body emanates the cosmos—including sky/earth, mind (*of being/non-being*), ego, senses, subtle elements, deities, Ṛg/Yajus/Sāman Vedas, time divisions, varṇas (Brāhmaṇa from mouth, etc.), and all movable/immovable beings.[^2][^3]

**Vṛhaspatitattva** opens with Śiva instructing Bṛhaspati on *tattvas* (principles of reality). Its central cosmogenic claim is ontological rather than narrative: the supreme *tattva* is eternally **twofold—*cetana* (Conscious Principle: inherently knowing, unforgetting; subdivided into Paramaśiva, Sadāśiva, Śiva) and *acetana* (Unconscious Principle: unknowing, like stone)**. Their conjunction (*cetana* pervading/weaving *acetana* as *ūtaprota*) generates all 36 *tattvas*: pure *cetana* (> Māyāśiras > Māyā [*acetana*] > stained ātmāns > pradhāna [*acetana* gross māyā*] > triguṇa [*sattva/rajas/tamas*] > buddhi > ahaṃkāra > 10 indriyas/manas > 5 tanmātras > 5 mahābhūtas). Śiva (*cetana*) animates this hierarchy; impure ātmāns forget due to māyā/pradhāna, binding them in saṃsāra.[^4][^5]

**Manuṣmṛti's framework would survive translation into Vṛhaspatitattva's idiom with the least damage.** Vṛhaspatitattva's *tattva*-ontology is a systematic grid that readily absorbs narrative cosmogonies like Manuṣmṛti's: Svayambhū/Brahmā maps to higher *cetana* (e.g., Sadāśiva/Śiva), the egg to māyā/pradhāna, mind/ego/indriyas/tanmātras/mahābhūtas to the lower 31 impure *tattvas*, and varṇas/beings to guṇa-bound births—all under *cetana*-acetana duality with Śiva as sovereign animator.[^6] Manuṣmṛti's monistic emanation (one principle unfolding all) aligns as *cetana* manifesting through *acetana*, preserving its hierarchy with minimal distortion.

The reverse strains more: Vṛhaspatitattva's eternal *cetana*-*acetana* duality (Śiva independent of māyā; ātmāns as distinct bound souls) clashes with Manuṣmṛti's unitary Self-Existent (no primordial unconscious rival; creation as self-projection from egg/Brahmā, reabsorbed into sleep/wake cycles). Forcing *acetana* into Manu's egg-motif loses Śaiva purity/impurity, ātman-multiplicity, and tattva-pervasion, reducing it to a flattened Vedic tale.[^7]

[^1]: Manusmṛti, Chapter 1, full search yields no "exhalation," "breath," or respiratory cosmogony (e.g., no *niśvāsa*); cf. later Purāṇic motifs absent here. (/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/)

[^2]: Key passage: "[p-61df11] This universe existed as darkness... [p-93cef0] Then the divine Self-existent One... [p-153021] That seed became a golden egg... in it he himself was born as Brahma..." (/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-61df11)

[^3]: Emanations: "[p-3bd729] ...split the egg... heavens and earth... [p-f689c4] ...mind... ego... [p-db3800] ...great soul... sense organs... [p-13a2db] ...Brahmana... from mouth..." (/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-3bd729)

[^4]: "[p-04d69c] The supreme *tattva* is twofold: *cetana* and *acetana*. It pervades all *tattvas*..." (/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-04d69c)

[^5]: Hierarchy/union: "*Cetana*... *Acetana*... When *cetana* and *acetana* meet, they give rise to all the *tattvas*: pradhānatattva... pañcamahābhūtatattva..." (/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-c31df3)

[^6]: E.g., Manu's mind/elements → Vṛhaspati's buddhi/ahaṃkāra/tanmātras/mahābhūtas; varṇas → guṇa births. (/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-464431)

[^7]: Manu's cycles: "[p-81b746] ...absorbed into his own Self... When that divine one is awake... world is active..." No room for eternal acetana/mala. (/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-81b746)
